entry.title: Customer COE Team Accreditation - From the customer's point of view
entry.content:
In customer projects, we often find that the success of implementing, using, and operating SAP products depends significantly on users having the necessary knowledge of the functionality of the deployed SAP solutions and upcoming product innovations, processes and their efficiency being transparent, and responsibilities being clearly defined. In addition, problems in the project and operation of SAP products are less of a technical nature and are mostly due to non-technical reasons, such as lack of solution acceptance, knowledge gaps, or communication problems.

This is precisely where the Customer COE concept from SAP comes into play: To establish an SAP team of experts across the business and SAP products that brings together the right resources and thus promotes a continuous exchange of information within the company.

In line with the SAP product portfolio, the Customer COE concept has also continued to evolve, but its intention has remained: to combine SAP expertise and competencies in the company and to bring togehter SAP experts across all areas. The resulting knowledge and network enable the Customer COE to continuously manage and drive improvements and innovations centrally – and the SAP product across and deployment independently.

 

Customer COE Team Accreditation from SAP - a summary

We have already reported in the Customer COE Community Blog on the added value of a Customer COE in the customer company and about the team accreditation mentioned in the same breath.

In summary, our team accreditation provides our customers every two years with guidance on which topics are well established as a foundation, which still need to be addressed, and which remain unanswered. Why every two years? Quite simply – nothing is more constant than change. A regular review is recommended, and every two years has proven to be a successful approach. Of course, an audit can also be requested during the year, for example, if business units are merged or other major organizational or technical changes occur. Here, too, preparing for team accreditation helps to sit down with the relevant SAP experts within the company - from IT and business - and coordinate the new structure.

In addition to the orientation of which topics are important, the result of the team accreditation can be used to measure the maturity of the basic criteria for a successful customer COE. In our experience, this is a good starting point for staying in motion in the continuous improvement process.

Another aspect is not to be underestimated, namely that of “internal marketing.” Too easily, the “stay on the ball” goes under and competes with prestigious projects. This is precisely where you should be supported by the certificate that you receive after a successful Customer COE Team accreditation and/or the top 10 customer award. In the following, we will discuss this again.

 

“Top10 Team Accreditation” - the perfect starting point to earn a gold star

As already mentioned, ongoing efforts and day-to-day improvements often go unnoticed, overshadowed by the spotlight on high-profile projects or urgent 'rescue missions' once problems have already escalated.

Let us celebrate and regularly showcase the achievements of your SAP expert team.

As part of the annual top 10 team accreditation, the audit results of a calendar year are evaluated globally. The winners of the “Top 10 Team Accreditation” achieved a very good overall result as part of the audit, some even the highest score – 200 out of 200 points. All Customer COE Top 10 winners in the respective calendar year will be contacted personally and will receive a Top10 certificate by post.

The very good performance of the Customer COE audit is a reason to celebrate. Take advantage of the award, pat yourself and your SAP team of experts on the back, and talk about what you can afford in everyday practice.

Use the Customer COE Top10 certificate and communicate your very good performance in your company. This is precisely the intention of the Top 10 award – in line with the principle "do good and tell people about it”.

Congratulations to all Customer COE Top10 winners of the past years!
You have decided to bring together SAP experts in the company to moderate the continuous change process, thus increasing transparency and efficiency in the implementation and the use of SAP software solutions and services.

In 2024, Customer COE were once again able to impress with their excellent audit results. The Customer COEs of the following companies have been recognized, to name a few:

BARMHERZIGE BRÜDER ÖSTERREICH, Cervecería Polar, C.A., Cotemar S.A. de C.V., Dataport, Evonik, Festo SE & Co. KG, KPMG IT Service GmbH, Nord/LB Norddeutsche Landesbank, RWE AG, Universität Basel, Vattenfall.

 

Experience of successful Customer COEs

Customer COEs – according to the intension of SAP – combine SAP expertise in the company and bring it together across all areas. In this way, the opportunities and challenges of emerging changes can be proactively shaped at an early stage

What have experienced Customer COEs learned, and how have regular team accreditations and other Customer COE services supported their success?

 

KPMG IT Service
“Accreditation offers us an excellent opportunity to work professionally with SAP at expert level, as well as to provide excellent support for our SAP applications and business processes and to develop them innovatively. The SAP award is a very great motivation for all our employees at KPMG Deutschland’s “SAP Center of Excellence”, a recognition of our performance, and proudly fulfils us.

Oliver Kuhne, Strategy & Service Management, KPMG IT Service GmbH
Top10 Winner 2024

Informations-Verarbeitungs-Zentrum (IVZ) /Kooperation von ARD und Deutschlandradio
“On the way to the S/4 world, we also reorganized ourselves and aligned directly with the Customer COE Framework. The framework and accompanying service from SAP Business Transformation (BT&A) helped us establish new collaboration models, strengthen governance, and strengthen processes, among other things. Meanwhile, we have also reached a very high degree of maturity here, which perfectly reflects the excellent result of our last team accreditation. But our journey is not over. The further expansion of cloud integrations and the inclusion of AI possibilities and the associated adaptation of our organization are only two of our future topics. “

Gil Ritter, SAP Applications, Head of Department / Customer COE Manager
Top 10 Winners 2024

Customer COE des Land Hessen (Hessisches Competence Center, HCC)
Customer COE of the German State of Hessen
SAP’s best-practice approach, now known as the Customer COE, has always provided us with important suggestions and decision support in setting up and developing our organization and processes in the HCC. It is a matter of course for us that we take the two-year Customer COE team accreditation, as we benefit greatly from it. On the one hand, the preparation contested by a large employee subgroup is a task that strongly promotes cohesion. With the activities, we also take stock of our activities over the past two years, as the audit measures our maturity level. On the other hand, there is the SAP Customer COE Quick Assessment Service (two-day workshop). Of course, we are on the test and are evaluated. However, we also receive incentives for reflection and sound comments, which in turn give us valuable impulses for our further development. Our serious approach has so far always been reflected in good results. The last six times, we each achieved the maximum number of points, which as a success of all employees also goes beyond the HCC.

Elisabeth Degoutrie, Head of Customer COE Management
Top 10 Winners 2023

Stadtwerke Potsdam GmbH
“We, from Stadtwerke Potsdam, are extremely proud and unbelievably happy to have received the award from SAP as one of the “Best 10 Customer COEs”. This honour is an exceptional recognition for the achievements of the entire team in recent years. Since 2004, we successfully undergo the certification/ team accreditation for SAP Customer COE every two years. The entire program provides us with valuable impulses regarding SAP’s strategy, new architecture concepts, and the modern operation of an SAP landscape. With these insights, we continuously evolve our organization and operations. Meanwhile, we have reached a highly professional level, for which we received this great award. It motivates all of us to continue along the path we have taken. “

Torsten Püplichhuisen, Head of Application Department, Stadwerke Potsdam
Top10 Winner 2023

Deutsche Rentenversicherung  (DRV)
“The SAP Customer COE program has always encouraged us to take new paths. No easy undertaking in the public sector. We are on the road with our Customer COE on these new paths – with agile approach, where it fits, with lived BizDevOps to realize our business requirements faster and with first steps to the cloud. The SAP Customer COE Team Accreditation (formerly certification), which lasts every two years, was awarded 200 points this time. It helps us – especially our customers – to position us as a competent team in the SAP environment.  Public service – and it’s all about it! “

Marko Stamatovic, IT domain personnel, German pension insurance
Top10 Winner 2023

regio iT gesellschaft für informationstechnologie mbh
“For us as a service provider, excellent service is very important. Our motto: “Delight customers! “. The renewed recognition of our SAP Customer COE in the top 10 is also important for our customers and a guarantee that you have chosen the right partner for all SAP matters and topics. For us! “

Daniel Geers, Center Manager SAP Platform & Operations, regio iT gesellschaft für informationtechnologie mbh
Top 10 Winners 2023

 

You’re curious now and want to start your own Customer COE journey?

Reach out to us anytime. In our Customer COE Portal Media Library, you can find support offerings for the setup and further development of your Customer COE under the chapter “Service”. Your SAP contacts and the SAP Customer COE program team  will be happy to help you find the right support for you.

entry.title: Re: An earlier article on 'SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls' receives an update
entry.content:
@ADLE 

Datasphere does not know about external users who use the same Datasphere database user account, so it can’t apply any additional filter, which forms the Data Access Control.

 

There needs to be a means for the application user to filter the data. For example, BusinessObjects enables a @Variable BOUSER and @Variable DBUSER, which are properties of the ‘application’ user. These values of the user are substituted as part of the SQL WHERE clause, for example. They often make use of a ‘permissions’ table to filter the data in very much a very similar way to ‘single value’ Datasphere Data Access Control does today. In the case with BusinessObjects, the target database has no idea about these other application users other than the user ID existing in the target database permissions table. For Datasphere, the same applies; an external application would need to filter the rows in a similar manner. Datasphere Data Access Controls are not applicable for application users, it is not aware of such users. Either apply a filter within the shared DB user session or make use of Datasphere Data Access Controls with a Datasphere DB user per application user.

 

I believe there is no ODBC/JDBC property that could pass through an application user within the database user login credentials. There is only a username, a password, a database, and a port number. If I’m correct, this means I believe it wouldn’t be possible for SAP or any other database vendor to support such a use case via the ODBC/JDBC interface.

Sorry, but I hope this helps at least to clarify what it is and what isn't possible.

Kind regards

Matthew

entry.title: Re: Custom data against SFC in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hi Ankit,

Unfortunately, as of today, the ability to insert custom fields at the SFC level has not yet been released.

However, I invite you to follow the SAP portal where you can suggest new features. Specifically, your request has already been added to the release list: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/320902.

I am assuming that you have more SFCs inside an order and each SFC has its vendor number.
To temporarily resolve the issue, I suggest creating dynamic data collection where you can link the object (vendor custom number) to the SFC.

Data Collections: Create a new DC Group with the name of your SFC and one parameter, "VENDORNUMBER," that is common across all data collection groups (names of other SFCs). You can create it dynamically during IDOC creation or set a trigger after the shop order release. With the new released SFC, you can create the data collection and attach the parameter (VENDORNUMBER). Then, fill the DC group with the vendor number (manually or automatically, based on the requirements).

entry.title: Digital Manufacturing - User Onboarding
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Digital Manufacturing plays a crucial role for various personas on the shop floor. During the implementation phase, the primary users are typically power users like Manufacturing Admins and Production Engineers. However, as you prepare for the go-live stage, it's essential to onboard Production Operators and Production Supervisors to the live system.

This onboarding process involves several key steps: setting up system access, assigning appropriate authorizations, and ensuring organizational alignment with plants and work centers.

In this blog, we'll provide an overview of these steps, along with practical hints to help you set everything up efficiently.

Business Use Case

To prepare for a new plant go-live, IT teams have some critical groundwork to cover. Their first step is to set up the required role collections based on input from the Business team. These role collections are then mapped to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication Tenant (IAS), which is a mandatory component in the overall solution architecture for using SAP Digital Manufacturing. 

Additionally, most customers have integrated SAP IAS with their corporate identity provider. In such cases, user creation and role assignments are managed directly within the corporate identity provider, with IAS acting as a proxy.

In this blog, we'll use the persona 'Worker' as an example to demonstrate the necessary steps for successful onboarding.

To ensure workers can perform their daily work in SAP Digital Manufacturing, they need to have the following things in place:

  • System access (authentication)
  • Authorizations to perform required actions
  • Organizational assignment, specifying the plant and work centers they're allowed to work in

Onboarding Steps

System Access (Authentication)

Ensuring secure access to Digital Manufacturing starts with authenticating users. SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication Tenant (IAS) provides multiple methods to enable secure system access. For detailed information, refer to SAP Help.

Depending on company policies and the chosen setup, new users, such as Production Operators, must receive personal credentials, like an email address and an initial password. In many companies, this process is initiated by the individuals responsible for hiring. It's managed through a central workflow that, once approved, creates the user either in the corporate identity provider or directly within IAS.

At this stage, users gain the ability to log in to SAP Digital Manufacturing. However, without assigned authorizations, they won't be able to perform actions or access any tiles in the system.

Authorizations

SAP Digital Manufacturing offers a variety of role templates that you can customize to create role collections tailored to specific business requirements. These templates consist of scopes and attributes, which serve as authorization objects. Details on available templates please refer to SAP Help

When users log in with an assigned role collection, SAP Digital Manufacturing evaluates the scopes within that collection. Based on this evaluation, users gain access to various apps (tiles on the launchpad) and receive authorizations, such as the ability to read or modify data and perform actions like raising an alert.

You manage the assignment of role collections within IAS by allocating user groups.

IAS – Group DMC_Operator assigned to Test Worker03

BTP – Role Collection DMC_OPERATOR mapped to User Groups DMC_OPERATOR:

Now, when users log on, the apps become visible on the launchpad. However, at this point, there is still no plant assignment configured. Without this critical organizational linkage, users won't have access to plant-specific data or functionality.

Assigning users to the appropriate plant is an important next step to ensure they can fully utilize the system and perform their designated tasks.

Organizational Assignment

Plant Assignment

The first step in the organizational assignment process is linking users to a plant. When it comes to assigning users with the role of Production_Operator to a plant, there are two possible approaches.

Later in the conclusion, we'll provide a comparison of the pros and cons for each option.

Option 1: Creation of the User in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This approach requires the involvement of a superuser who has authorization to manage user assignments, such as those with roles like Production_Supervisor, Production_Engineer, or Manufacturing_Admin.

To create users within the Manage User Assignments application in SAP Digital Manufacturing, superusers can choose from the following methods:

  • Manual creation through the user interface: Adding individual users directly through the interface.
  • Importing a CSV File: Uploading bulk user data in a structured format.
  • Creation using a public API: Automating user creation through an external API integration.

It's essential that the superuser responsible for assigning the plant is located within the plant they intend to assign the new users to. Additionally, when creating users, you must use their email addresses as their user IDs.

Option 2: Assignment of the Plant_Access Role

Another method for assigning users to a plant involves using the Plant_Access role template.
When users are granted the custom-defined Plant_Access role and log into SAP Digital Manufacturing for the first time, they can select a plant from the plant selection menu at the top-right corner of the screen. By choosing a plant, users set it as their current plant. This action simultaneously triggers their automatic creation as users within that plant in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

The Plant_Access role also restricts the selection of plants available to users. This limitation ensures that users can only choose from plants specifically maintained within the role, which is a significant benefit for managing access efficiently.

For detailed information on the Plant_Access role, refer to SAP Help.
Important Notes:

  • For users with the Production_Operator role, there is no functional difference between plant attributes set as Plant_Read or Plant_Manage, as the Production_Operator role only provides read access to plant data.
  • The Plant_Access role does not provide additional authorizations; it solely restricts access according to predefined parameters. Therefore, it must be paired with a persona-based role. 

Sample role based on Plant_Access template:

Authorization assigned via User Groups in IAS:

After successfully creating users and linking them to the plant, the next step is assigning work centers. This assignment is crucial, as it determines the specific areas within the plant where users can operate.

If Production Operators attempt to access a Production Operator Dashboard (POD) and execute a shop floor control (SFC), the system will automatically verify their assigned work centers. Without this assignment, they will not be able to perform operations or access related functionalities.

Work Center Assignment

When it comes to assigning work centers to users, two methods are available:

1. Manual assignment within the Manage User Assignments app 


2. Automated assignment using the provided public APIs available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

While the manual process works well for small-scale onboarding, it becomes increasingly time-consuming when you need to onboard multiple Production Operators. To streamline this process, investing time in building a custom application that leverages public APIs can be a game-changer.

Conclusion

Streamlining the Onboarding Process with Combined Methods.
To enhance the efficiency of the onboarding process while ensuring secure plant access, you can effectively combine the assignment of the Plant_Access role with user creation via API or by a superuser.

By creating users as part of the onboarding process—either through API automation or manually by the superuser—you eliminate the dependency on Production Operators logging in first to trigger their creation in the system. This proactive approach ensures smoother and faster onboarding.

Simultaneously, assigning the Plant_Access role serves as an additional safeguard. Even in the case of an error by the superuser, the Plant_Access role restricts Production Operators' plant selection to only those defined within the role, thereby maintaining secure and controlled access.

entry.title: Re: How to chain HANA SQL input files in a HANA SQL Input file
entry.content:
Andreas, I was able to recreate the same situation, where HDBSQL switches to promptless command input after executing the first sql script from the list of scripts. You can even type `\q;` to get it exiting that "hanging" mode.

I am going to check that with the development team, but in the meantime would a shell script similar to this work for you?

#!/usr/bin/env bash
read -sp "Enter password: " pswd
echo
hdbsql -n b7c3ff95-9e0c-480d-9022-bfaa268f780.hna0.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443 -u DBADMIN -p ${pswd} -xaj -I step1.sql
hdbsql -n b7c3ff95-9e0c-480d-9022-bfaa268f780.hna0.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443 -u DBADMIN -p ${pswd} -xaj -I step2.sql
hdbsql -n b7c3ff95-9e0c-480d-9022-bfaa268f780.hna0.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443 -u DBADMIN -p ${pswd} -xaj -I step3.sql

Regards,
--Vitaliy

entry.title: Re: How to chain HANA SQL input files in a HANA SQL Input...
entry.content:
I hope I have a solution for you @andreas_zigann , but I still would need to validate it with the engineering team. Please, try to change you `configure_hana.script` to:

\read create_users.sql;

entry.title: Re: Preparing for SAP Identity Management’s End-of-Maintenance in 2027
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

This is an interesting and very relevant discussion. As an IDM consultant, I'm currently working with clients who are facing the same endpoint regarding the upcoming end of maintenance for SAP IDM and are actively exploring replacement options.

Following this thread, I also have some questions and would like to share my initial thoughts and a challenge we are encountering with Microsoft Entra ID as a potential successor to SAP IDM 8.0.

While Microsoft Entra ID offers a robust cloud-based identity platform, we are finding it's not immediately clear if it can fully replicate all the granular functionalities we've come to rely on in SAP IDM 8.0. The flexibility and customization within SAP IDM for complex, on-premises SAP environments are significant.

In our current SAP IDM environment, we often leverage SQL and JavaScript to generate custom reports or tailor workflows to precisely meet business requirements. However, a significant shift we are now encountering is the need to utilize PowerShell for similar tasks within Active Directory when considering Entra ID integration.

One specific challenge we've encountered relates to access management. While I have experience with Microsoft Entra ID, our company policy presents a hurdle in granting SAP IDM consultants the level of Microsoft system administrator access that might be necessary for deep integration and management. This stems from fundamentally different security concepts between the SAP and Microsoft ecosystems. Allowing consultants with deep SAP knowledge to have broad administrative rights within our core Microsoft infrastructure raises security concerns, which I understand.

I am curious if other SAP IDM consultants are facing similar challenges regarding the shift to PowerShell for AD management or have found effective strategies to navigate this security and access paradigm when considering Microsoft Entra ID as a replacement. If so, I would greatly appreciate it if you could share your experiences and any best practices you've discovered.

Thank you for this informative discussion and for any insights you can provide.

Best regards,

S

entry.title: Re: AI-based anomaly monitoring using SAP Payroll Control Center
entry.content:
Hi Diego @dvera ,

To identify outliers, one could send payroll data to an SAP BTP API. For alerts, a new validation rule could be developed based on a wage type, and the event listener could be used to create them.

Consider a simple scenario where an API service developed in Python using libraries such as Scikit-learn or PyTorch receives employee wage types in JSON format and returns outliers in the same format. Outliers could be found using the Isolation Forest technique.

The Python project could be deployed to SAP BTP via Cloud Foundry, ensuring the service is active.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-cf-buildpacks-python-create..html

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/build-a-python-app-on-btp-cloud-foundry-read-filter-and-download-table-data/ba-p/13898362

A basic ABAP program could be developed, making use of the method CREATE_BY_URL of class IF_HTTP_CLIENT.

Running the payroll simulation with a Payroll Control Center monitoring process and running this new ABAP program could pass the payroll data and receive the API results. It could use infotype 0015 to assign a specific wage type to outlier employees. It's worth noting that the event listener handling outliers generates alerts for these personnel, which show automatically on PCC.

By using several machine learning methods after this initial test, the outlier search could be improved.

Please bear in mind that this is just a proof-of-concept notion, as other elements of EC Payroll and SAP BTP security need to be considered. SAP aims to make it easier for SuccessFactors users to use AI tools.

Thank you for your interest in this. Hopefully, the information will be helpful.

Regards,
Jose

entry.title: SAP RAP Custom entity referential constraint error with composition and @OData.property.name
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm currently working on an SAP RAP unmanaged scenario using Custom Entities in ABAP Cloud, and I’ve run into an issue when trying to expose metadata.

Scenario:

I have two custom entities defined as follows:

Root Entity:

 
@EndUserText.label: 'Custom Root entity for Parent'
define root custom entity ZParent {
  @OData.property.name: 'SessionId'
  key SESSION_ID : abap.int1;

  _Child : composition [1..1] of ZChild;
}

Child Entity:

 

 
@EndUserText.label: 'Custom entity for Child'
define custom entity ZChild {
  @OData.property.name: 'SessionId'
  key SESSION_ID : abap.int1;

  _Parent : association to parent ZParent on _Parent.SESSION_ID = $projection.SESSION_ID;
}

Issue:

When I try to access the $metadata of the service, I receive the following error:

Code: SY/530  
Message: Association assoc_0FCF7701A629F6O035FFE9E6E21380F1: referential constraint property not found in entity ZChildType.

Interestingly, the error does not occur when the @odata.property.name annotation is removed.

Question:

Does the RAP framework not automatically handle property name mapping when using @odata.property.name?

Is there an annotation or recommended approach to handle referential constraints in this case?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks & Regards,
Aftab

entry.title: Re: insufficient privilege: error while preview the calcu...
entry.content:
Hello,

Why even SYSTEM User Fails?
SYSTEM user has DB-level rights, but analytic privilege checks happen above database level at the calculation engine layer.  SAP HANA enforces these separately for data security in views. 

In SAP HANA, even SYSTEM users must have analytic privileges to read/view data from Calculation Views (CUBE or DIMENSION types, especially). Privileges like SELECT on the base tables alone are not enough.

Calculation Views require ANALYTIC PRIVILEGES or SQL Analytic Privileges to be assigned.

 

  • Go to HANA Studio → Security → Analytic Privileges.

  • Create a new Analytic Privilege or use an existing one.

  • It must include authorization to access the specific Calculation View

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Not able to locate saved data in custom table of SAP...
entry.content:
Hello Rutuja,

 

  • Is the behavior implementation saving data to the table you expect (e.g., ZCPR_ITEM_DB_01)?

  • Or is there a separate internal buffer or temp entity used before it hits the actual persistent table?

Nevertheless, 
If you want to see where data is actually going, just add a breakpoint in your behavior class (ZBP_R_CPR_ITEM_DB_01) → create method.
Upload again, and you’ll see exactly what’s being passed to the table and whether modify or insert is called.

 

OR

Verify where the data is actually going in Eclispe :-

SELECT * FROM zcpr_item_db_01 INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data).
cl_demo_output=>display( lt_data ).

If it returns nothing, try looking at the generated runtime class (like ZR_CPR_ITEM_DB_01), and debug the create call to see what table it's writing to.

Please let me know if you need anything else.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Encryption Key - Database - SSFS
entry.content:
When you run:


rsecssfx generatekey -getPlainValueToConsole
It generates a random symmetric key used for internal encryption purposes in SAP (example: for credentials, connection passwords).

generated key is 256-bit AES key (32 bytes, hex-encoded).

Typically printed as a 64-character hexadecimal string (each byte = 2 hex digits).

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Email output completed in system but not sent from sa...
entry.content:
Hi,

Screen shots would have helped but without it, these are just guesses.

Please check if the email channel was enabled in output parameter determination and if it has been activated. In the manage billing document app, under the output items check if the output is related to the email channel or just IDOC. Also, check to confirm that exclusive indicator is not set on the previous channels in the list.

If the email channel is not showing up then it was not configured right. You can also check the monitor email transmission app to see if the item was created for the output. 

Please review https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/c9b98e682223453b9185d11de90670ac.html as well.

If all of these are in place and its still not working, you can raise a ticket to SAP

 

Regards,

Dennis

entry.title: Re: Custom tile & catalog in S/4HANA Public Cloud (2SL-SB)
entry.content:
In 2SL, you cannot create a truly "new" custom catalog; you can only extend very limited fields or work inside a Developer Extensibility framework.

Alternatives :- 

1. Use a separate portal or SAP Build Work Zone

  • You could expose an SAP Build Work Zone or SAP BTP Launchpad Service.

  • Create a tile there that points to SAP IAG.

  • Users would only access BTP Portal, not S/4 directly → no S/4 license needed until assigned.

2. SAP IAG Link Outside S/4 (Email / External Site)

  • Send new hires an email with a link directly to SAP IAG.

  • After approval and role provisioning is complete, then they get their S/4 Launchpad access.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Oracle DB TCPS Connection issue - Integration Suite
entry.content:
I am trying to connect to an On-Prem Oracle 19c DB in Integration Suite. 
Cloud Connector connectivity is fine.
I tried the following:
 
1. JDBC URL provided (Old style Full Address format):
This URL was provided in the JDBC Material, Oracle driver was also installed
 pod1234.test-testing.com is a virtual host
 
jdbc:oracle:thin:@(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=tcps)(HOST=pod1234.test-testing.com)(PORT=1539))(CONNECT_DATA=(SERVICE_NAME=TEST_ABC3)))
 
Outcome: fails with error com.sap.it.rt.adapter.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCException: Error while getting Datasource Service: ServiceReference is null
 
2. Tried EZCONNECT Style since I think Full Address format is not supported: 
a) jdbc:oracle:thin:@tcps:pod1234.test-testing.com:1539/TEST_ABC3?oracle.net.ssl_server_cert_dn="CN=pod1234a"
b) jdbc:oracle:thin:@tcps://pod1234.test-testing.com:1539/TEST_ABC3?oracle.net.ssl_server_cert_dn="CN=pod1234a"
 
Outcome: fails with the same error com.sap.it.rt.adapter.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCException: Error while getting Datasource Service: ServiceReference is null
 
The following URL worked fine: 
3. jdbc:oracle:thin:@pod1234.test-testing.com:1521/TEST_ABC3
but this is using the TCP protocol, and I want to connect using the TCPS protocol.
 
Please note that the cloud connector configuration is working fine; I don't think there is a problem here.
Additionally, I had also uploaded the certificate required for the TCPS connection SSL handshake in the Monitor > "Integration and APIs" > Keystore.
 
In the local system using DB Client DataGrip, I was able to connect using all the above URLs, but in Integration Suite, I am getting the error.
 
Please suggest what could be the issue.

entry.title: Updating Dimension Attributes in a model
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I request some help with a requirement to incorporate YTD Gross values from external files into tax calculations. Currently, YTD values are derived from monthly wages in the system, but we now need to use the YTD from the file for the last month of each quarter.

  1. Flow:

    • For March 2025, overwrite the YTD value with the file’s YTD.

    • For April 2025, calculate YTD as (March’s file YTD + April’s SAC-calculated wage).

  2. Approach Followed:

    • Step 1: Load the file into the “Actual” version, mapping Employee ID, Period, and YTD to dimensions/measures. Default values (e.g., 1900-01-01, #) are used for missing attributes like dates, company code, shift, etc.

    • Step 2: Use a data action to copy from “Actual” to “Working Forecast,” assigning dimension values from master data.

    • Step 3: Shift data is pulled from a different model using LINK (since it’s absent in master data).

  3. Issue: Company code is not in master data but exists in the same model under a different data source. Attempts to use RESULTLOOKUP failed.

Code Snippet:

// Define Membersets
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "SALARY"
MEMBERSET [d/GL_ACCOUNT] = ("EMP_SALARY")
MEMBERSET [d/MEASURE_TYPE] = "M00"
MEMBERSET [d/TIME] = %CutOffMonth%
 
// 1. Copy value from Actual/PROLL_YTD_GROSS to CALC-REG/SS_YTD5
DATA(
// Target Context
[d/DATASRC] = "CALC-REG", [d/GL_ACCOUNT] = "SS_YTD5", [d/TIME] = PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/TIME]),
// Populate Dimensions from Master Data
[d/START_DATE] = [d/EMPLOYEE].[p/ST_DATE_FORMATTED], [d/COSTCENTER] = [d/EMPLOYEE].[p/COSTCENTER],
[d/EMPSTAT] = [d/EMPLOYEE].[p/POS_STAT],
// Explicit Empty Dimensions
[d/END_DATE] = "", [d/TERM_DATE] = ""
) =
ROUND(
  RESULTLOOKUP(
   [d/VERSION] = "public.Actual", [d/DATASRC] = "PROLL_YTD_GROSS",
   [d/TIME] = PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/TIME]), [d/SHIFT] = "#"
  ), 2)
* ((
LINK(
[SHIFT_MAPPING], [d/Version] = "public.Update Shift", [d/Date] = "202301", [d/EMP_CLASS] = "#",
[d/EMP_TYPE] = "#", [d/SHIFT_CODE] = "#", [d/WORK_SCHEDULE] = "#"
) * 0
   ) + 1)
 
Any inputs would be greatly helpful.
 
Thanks in Advance,
Phani

entry.title: Error while connecting sap UI5 Application in BAS using Firebase
entry.content:
this is the error

 

 

This is Index.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">

    <script src="https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/7.14.5/firebase-database.js"></script>
    <script src="https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/7.23.0/firebase-storage.js"></script>
    <script src="https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/7.8.0/firebase-functions.js"></script>
    <script>
        // Your Firebase configuration
        var firebaseConfig = {
            apiKey: "",
                authDomain: "",
                projectId: "",
                storageBucket: "",
                messagingSenderId: "",
                appId: ""
        };
               firebase.initializeApp(firebaseConfig);
    </script>
</head>
<body class="sapUiBody sapUiSizeCompact" id="content">
    <div
        data-sap-ui-component
        data-name="firebaselogin"
        data-id="container"
        data-settings='{"id" : "firebaselogin"}'
        data-handle-validation="true"
    ></div>
</body>
</html>

This is component.js

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/UIComponent",
    "firebaselogin/model/models",
    "firebaselogin/js/Firebase"
], (UIComponent, models, Firebase) => {
    "use strict";

    return UIComponent.extend("firebaselogin.Component", {
        metadata: {
            manifest: "json",
            interfaces: [
                "sap.ui.core.IAsyncContentCreation"
            ]
        },

        init() {
            // call the base component's init function
            UIComponent.prototype.init.apply(this, arguments);

            // set the device model
            this.setModel(models.createDeviceModel(), "device");

            // enable routing
            this.getRouter().initialize();

            //set Firebase Model
            this.setModel(Firebase.initializeFirebase(), "fbModel");

            
        }
    });
});

this is firebase.js

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
], function (JSONModel) {
    "use strict";
    return {
        // Firebase-config retrieved from the Firebase-console
        initializeFirebase: function () {
            // Replace with your config here
            

            const firebaseConfig = {
                apiKey: "",
                authDomain: "",
                projectId: "",
                storageBucket: "",
                messagingSenderId: "",
                appId: ""
               
              };


            // Initialize Firebase with the Firebase-config
            firebase.initializeApp(firebaseConfig);

            // Create a Firestore reference
            const firestore = firebase.firestore();

            // Create a Authentication reference
            const fireAuth = firebase.auth();

            // Get Firebase Instance
            const oFirestore = firebase.firestore;

            // Create a Fire Storage reference
            const fireStorage = firebase.storage();

            // Create a Fire Functions reference
            const fireFunctions = firebase.app().functions('asia-east2');

            // Firebase services object
            const oFirebase = {
                firestore: firestore,
                fireAuth: fireAuth,
                oFirestore: oFirestore,
                fireStorage: fireStorage,
                fireFunctions: fireFunctions
            };

            // Create a Firebase model out of the oFirebase service object which contains all required Firebase services
            var fbModel = new JSONModel(oFirebase);

            // Return the Firebase Model
            return fbModel;
        }
    };
});

this is view1.view.xml

<mvc:View controllerName="firebaselogin.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m">
    <pages>
                <Page id="page" title="Email and Password Login Integration in SAP UI5 using Google Firebase">
                    <content>
                        <VBox height="100%" alignItems="Center">
                            <HBox height="100%" alignItems="Center">
                                <Image height="100px" class="sapUiLargeMarginEnd"
                                    src="https://www.loc-online.co.uk/berkshire-loc/wp-content/uploads/sites/23/2020/06/Screenshot-2020-05-28-at-16.35.37-266x300.png"/>
                                <IconTabBar id="idIconTabBarNoIcons" expanded="{device>/isNoPhone}" selectedKey="mail" class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding">
                                    <items>
                                        <IconTabFilter text="Email Login" key="mail">
                                            <VBox id="idMailDetails">
                                                <Label design="Bold" text="Enter Registered Email ID" required="true"/>
                                                <Input width="300px" id="idu_admin" required="true" placeholder="username@mail.com" submit="onEmailSignin"/>
                                                <Label design="Bold" text="Enter Password" required="true"/>
                                                <Input width="300px" id="idp_admin" required="true" type="Password" placeholder="*****" submit="onEmailSignin"/>
                                                <HBox>
                                                    <Button width="145px" class="sapUiTinyMarginEnd" text="Sign In" press="onEmailSignin"/>
                                                    <Button width="145px" text="Reset" press="onReset"/>
                                                </HBox>
                                            </VBox>
                                        </IconTabFilter>
                                    </items>
                                </IconTabBar>
                            </HBox>
                        </VBox>
                    </content>
                </Page>
                </pages>
</mvc:View>

this is view1 controller

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
], (Controller) => {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("firebaselogin.controller.View1", {
        onInit() {
            
        },
        onEmailSignin: function (oEvent) {
            var that = this;
            sap.ui.core.BusyIndicator.show();
            var email = this.byId("idu_admin").getValue();
            var password = this.byId("idp_admin").getValue();
            var errorMessage = "";
            // Create a Fireauth Auth reference
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel("fbModel").getData();
            var fireAuth = oModel.fireAuth;
            // var firestoreData = oModel.firestore;
            fireAuth.signInWithEmailAndPassword(email, password).then(function (usersigned) {
                sap.ui.core.BusyIndicator.hide();
                MessageBox.success("You are Logged in!");
                that.onReset();
            }).catch(function (error) {
                sap.ui.core.BusyIndicator.hide();
                // Handle Errors here.
                errorMessage = error.message;
                MessageBox.error(errorMessage);
            });
        },

        onReset: function (oEvent) {
            this.byId("idu_admin").setValue("");
            this.byId("idp_admin").setValue("");
        }
    });
});

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 中的新增功能：MIGO对生产订单/预留投料可自动确定仓库仓位（Bin位）
entry.content:
在支持公有云客户的过程中，收到好几家使用了cloud WM的公有云客户有直接MIGO对生产订单进行组件投料并确定Bin位的业务需求。事实上在使用MIGO对生产订单进行组件投料的时候，系统有库存确定和批次确定的功能，但在之前的系统版本中却不能自动推荐库存所在仓库存储的物理Bin位，这对用户的操作造成了不便，同时也不利于仓库货物移动效率的提升。

在收到客户诉求反馈后，ERP公有云cloud WM产品团队评估了客户提出此诉求的合理性和优先级，经过产品开发，从ERP公有云2502.2版本开始，启用了cloud WM的公有云客户，可以直接在MIGO中对生产订单投料的时候由系统推荐并确定Bin位（具体参数：MIGO，移动类型261，对生产订单或者预留进行直接发货过账操作），详细功能请参考以下功能测试结果。请注意以下截屏为内部系统测试数据，具体测试结果请以客户系统实际为准。

首先在内部系统中创建一张生产订单并发布以待发料，组件选择从WM管理库存地点发料，如下显示：

其次进入MIGO界面投料：

 

 

 

 

点击post，发货过账成功，查看结果如下：

 

查看仓库管理监控器中的仓库库存变化：

 

请注意：仓库自动确定并推荐批次需要完善仓库管理相关配置，也可定义出库规则，如先进先出、后进先出等，系统根据配置的库位搜索策略找到合适的库存。具体配置如下：

 

 

 

 

 

若生产订单中BOM组件物料维护了production supply area，同时该物料也维护了control cycle for WM（bin位字段维护值），则MIGO默认对生产订单组件发料时可带出维护的默认Bin位，对默认Bin位不满意可以在MIGO中使用以上auto bin的功能由系统根据规则自动确定Bin位，若对推荐的Bin位仍不满意，可以手工维护优先级更高的Bin位，进行组件的最终发料过账。需要注意的是，手工指认Bin位的同时也需要对批次进行相应的更新。

目前公有云系统中cloud WM仓库管理功能已非常强大，不管是物料的物理货架管理，还是仓库的出入库流程、手持终端扫码需求抑或是系统集成需求，都已有相应并完善的产品功能进行支持。推荐有仓库管理需求的客户优先使用ERP公有云中自带的免费仓库管理功能（cloud WM），在使用过程中，有产品功能相关反馈欢迎与我们联系，谢谢。

关于以上提到的MIGO auto-bin功能，更多功能更新说明文档，可参考SAP Help文档：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 中的新增功能：直接发货过账期间的自动仓位确定

entry.title: Exactly once in order (EOIO) for Group messages using Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi there,

We have recently introduced SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM), which under the hood is ‘Solace PubSub+ Cloud’ in our Integration Landscape, and now I am seeking for the best way to implement ‘Exactly Once In Order’ Sequencing in our Integration.

We are on Version 10.9.1.114-0.

The Integration is going to do the following:

  1. Source System will Publish the Switching Job details (XML) to SAP AEM from WebLogic JMS Server.
  2. We have another SAP Integration Layer which then subscribes to the Solace Queue (or Topic), does Message Transformation, and then forwards that to External System.

The Challenge:

We want messages belonging to one Job (lets say Job A), should be delivered to External System in Sequence. Lets say Job A has Operation 1, 2 and 3. If for some reason Operation 2 fails to get delivered to External System, Job 3 should not go. However, Job B and it’s Operations can continue to go as usual.

So, we need FIFO for individual Job Operation, and not across all the Job Operations.

Analysis done so far:

  1. We can create One Exclusive Queue and One DMQ for the process, and Publish everything there. By doing it all the Jobs and their Operations will follow Sequence by Default. In All the Success scenarios, this should be okay (I guess). But in case of failure, the message will move to DMQ and the Subsequent message for one Job will not know and ‘might’ get processed.
  2. I went through Partition Queues, but that doesn’t seem to fit the purpose, as there the messages (Job Operations in our case) of one Job can go to any Partition
  3. Non-Durable (Temporary) Queues: What I have understood so far, we can request the Publisher (Client) to create Queues Dynamically and all the messages (Operation) of one Job should be sent to ONE Queue. As soon as those messages are consumed by our SAP Integration and forwarded to Externally System, these Queues get deleted - I am not 100% sure if this is how this will behave. In case of failures, I would expect the Queue remains intact and messages belonging to that Queue will pile up, without impacting the other Jobs.
  4. We can introduce a persistence layer to store the failed messages. Every message consumed from the queue will first be checked (using some checksum) to see if any message for that Operation has failed in the past and if yes, the push the message to DMQ or else continue normal. I will then have to keep DMQ and this persistence layer in sync. Or just use this persistence and not DMQ.

Following things are Mandatory:

  1. Guaranteed Delivery of the messages to External System
  2. Order of Operations belonging to same Job should be maintained
  3. Order for Different Jobs is not Mandatory, and those can be parallelly processed.

Any help on this would be really appreciated.

-Anuj

entry.title: Re: How to manage Start Retrofit action under Transport M...
entry.content:
To disable the "Start Retrofit" option in the Transport Management Assignment block within SAP Solution Manager (SOLMAN) Change Control Management, follow these steps:

  1. Access SAP Configuration:

    • Go to transaction SM34 and open the view cluster /TMWFLOW/VC_CTS_DIS.

  2. Adjust Retrofit Settings:

    • Within the cluster, locate your CTS Project Settings or Retrofit Settings.

    • Choose the relevant project or landscape and uncheck the Retrofit Enabled option or similar setting that activates the retrofit feature.

  3. Save and Transport:

    • Save your changes and transport them if needed, depending on your system landscape.

  4. Refresh the UI or Restart Application:

    • A UI refresh or restarting services (e.g., via SICF) may be required for the changes to take full effect.

Once completed, the "Start Retrofit" option will no longer be visible or accessible in the Transport Management block.

Just like professionals seek CMI Level 6 Assignment Help to navigate complex academic content, customizing SAP Solution Manager features like this often benefits from precise, guided steps to avoid confusion and ensure correct configuration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Group Reporting - Block tasks in Consolidation an...

Hi,

All tasks in the data monitor must be blocked before the period can be closed. Tasks with errors or warnings need to be locked with block by user. Same applies for the consolidation monitor.

entry.title: Creation of SAP HANA Database Instance in SAP BTP Trial
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP HANA Cloud is a database as a service (DBaaS) , provides a single place to access, store, and process all enterprise data in real time with high availability .You can develop smart applications with embedded machine learning , analytics (advanced data processing capabilities , insights ) ,document store . SAP HANA Cloud provides all of the advanced SAP HANA technologies for multi-model data processing ,store and access data in-memory and column-based [ it Process mission-critical data at proven in-memory speed] or on disk . By combining in-memory storage with columnar store, data operations are performed faster than in a traditional database with a disk-based storage. Using SAP HANA Cloud, you can set up and manage SAP HANA databases and bind them to applications running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) or elsewhere. You can benefit from cloud qualities such as availability, security, automatic software updates, elasticity, and low total cost of ownership by using SAP HANA Cloud either as a stand-alone solution or as an extension to your existing on-premise environment.

1. Open SAP BTP  Cockpit and click on subaccount.

2. Click on Instances and Subscriptions and then click on create button. 

3. Select Service as SAP HANA Cloud and Plan as tools. 

4. SAP HANA Cloud is now subscribed. 

5. Click on Security -> Users and select the user. 

6. Click on Assign Role Collection. 

7. Assign the following three roles. 

8. Click on SAP HANA Cloud Application; it will open in new window(Hana Cloud Central). 

9. Click on create Instance. 

10. Select SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database and Next Step 

11. Provide the instance name and password (next step). 

12. Click on Next Step. 

13. Click on Next Step. 

14. Select Allow all IP addresses, Enable Cloud Connector, and select Allow all IP addresses --> Next Step. 

15. Click on Review and Create(No need to select Data Lake). 

16. Click on Create Instance. 

17. Wait until the Instance status turns into Running state and click on Actions three dots. 

18. Go to Open in SAP HANA Database Explorer; it will open in new window. 

  1. Enter the Credentials 

Username: DBADMIN   

Password: “use the password which was created at previous steps”. 

  1. Click on SQL Console Icon at the top left. 

Now the SAP HANA Database is ready to use. 

Conclusion:

Here in this post I have completed only setting up Hana Cloud Instance, but this is only the first part of my blog. I will be completing my blogs by working on Hana Database Integration HDI, schemas and tables in Hana Database Instance. I will be posting this in my up coming blog.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere CLI & Python: Exporting Modeling Objects to CSV Files for Each Artifact
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, we'll explore how to use Python alongside SAP Datasphere CLI to extract modeling objects and export them to CSV files. The script allows users to handle artifacts such as remote tables, views, replication flows, and more, for each space in SAP Datasphere.
This solution is particularly useful for automating repetitive tasks and ensuring structured data handling across different modeling objects

Prerequisites

Steps to install SAP Datasphere CLI: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/d0ecd6f297ac40249072a44df0549c1a/f7d5eddf20a34a1aa48d8e2c68a44e28.html/ 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-datasphere-external-access-overview-apis-cli-and-sql/bc-p/14086942#M180986/ 

Step-by-Step Process

Step 1: Prepare Login.Json file

Create OAuth Client with Purpose as Interactive Usage and Redirect URL as http://localhost:8080

Get the value of all below fields from the OAuth Client and prepare the Login.json file.

{
"client_id": "",
"client_secret": "",
"authorization_url": "",
"token_url": "",
"access_token": "",
"refresh_token": ""
}

 Step 2: Create Model_Object.py file with below code

dsp host : give URL of Datasphere Tenant.

secrets_file : Give Path of Login.json file.

import subprocess
import pandas as pd
import sys

def manage_Modeling_Object(Modeling_Object):
    # Step 1: Login to Datasphere using host and secrets file
    dsp_host = '<URL of Datasphere>'
    secrets_file = '<path>/Login.json'
    command = f'datasphere login --host {dsp_host} --secrets-file {secrets_file}'
    subprocess.run(command, shell=True)  # Execute the login command
    
    # Step 2: Retrieve a list of all spaces in JSON format
    command = ['datasphere', 'spaces', 'list', '--json']
    result_spaces = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, shell=True, text=True)  # Run the command and capture output
    
    # Step 3: Parse the list of spaces from the command's output
    spaces = result_spaces.stdout.splitlines()  # Split output into individual lines
    
    ModelingObject_data = []  # Initialize a list to store Modeling Object data
    
    # Step 4: Check if the Modeling Object is 'spaces'
    if Modeling_Object == 'spaces':
        for space in spaces:
            if space == "[" or space == "]":
                continue  # Skip brackets in the JSON output
            space_id = space.strip()  # Extract space ID
            
            # Add space details to the data list
            ModelingObject_data.append({
                'Space ID': space_id.replace('"', '').replace(',', ''),
                'Technical Name': space_id.replace('"', '').replace(',', ''),
                'TYPE': Modeling_Object[:-1].upper()  # Set the TYPE as uppercase version of the input Modeling Object name
            })
    
    # Step 5: Process Modeling Objects for each space
    else:
        for space in spaces:
            if space == "[" or space == "]":
                continue  # Skip brackets in the JSON output
            space_id = space.strip()  # Extract space ID
            
            # Step 6: Retrieve Modeling Objects for the current space
            command = ['datasphere', 'objects', Modeling_Object, 'list', '--space', space_id.replace('"', '').replace(',', '')]
            result_ModelingObject = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, shell=True, text=True)  # Run the command
            
            # Step 7: Parse the Modeling Object data from the output
            ModelingObject_info = result_ModelingObject.stdout.splitlines()  # Split output into individual lines
            print("Checking "+Modeling_Object.upper()+" for space : "+space_id.replace('"', '').replace(',', ''))  # Log the space being checked
            
            # Step 8: Process each Modeling Object
            if len(ModelingObject_info) > 1:
                for flow in ModelingObject_info:
                    if '{' in flow or '}' in flow or '[' in flow or ']' in flow:
                        continue  # Skip brackets or braces in the output
                    cleaned_flow = flow.replace('"technicalName":', '').replace('"', '').strip()  # Clean up the output
                    
                    # Step 9: Add Modeling Object details to the data list
                    ModelingObject_data.append({
                        'Space ID': space_id.replace('"', '').replace(',', ''),
                        'Technical Name': cleaned_flow,
                        'TYPE': Modeling_Object[:-1].upper()  # Set the TYPE as uppercase version of the input Modeling Object name
                    })
    
    # Step 10: Write the collected data into a CSV file
    if ModelingObject_data:
        df = pd.DataFrame(ModelingObject_data)  # Create a DataFrame from the data list
        df.to_csv(Modeling_Object.upper()+'.csv', index=False)  # Save the DataFrame to a CSV file without the index
        print("Space vise all "+Modeling_Object.upper()+" have been written to "+Modeling_Object.upper()+".csv.")  # Log success message
    else:
        print("No Modeling Objects found.")  # Log message if no data was collected
    
    print('------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------')  # Separator for readability
        
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Check if an argument is provided via the command line
    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
        # Pass the first argument to the method
        manage_Modeling_Object(sys.argv[1])
    else:
        print("Please provide a Modeling Object name as an argument.")  # Log error message if no argument is provided
        
# Execute for predefined Modeling Objects
manage_Modeling_Object('remote-tables')
manage_Modeling_Object('local-tables')
manage_Modeling_Object('views')
manage_Modeling_Object('intelligent-lookups')
manage_Modeling_Object('data-flows')
manage_Modeling_Object('replication-flows')
manage_Modeling_Object('transformation-flows')
manage_Modeling_Object('task-chains')
manage_Modeling_Object('analytic-models')
manage_Modeling_Object('data-access-controls')

Step 3: Open command prompt and execute the Model_Objects.py file

Once the program execution is done it will generate CSV files for all the Datasphere artifactes mention in python code

each CSV file will have 3 columns : 

1) Space ID : Name of the space

2) Technical Name : Exact Technical Name of Object 

3) Type : Type of Object ( i.e view, local-table, remote-table, replication flw etc)

Conclusion

This script demonstrates how Python and SAP Datasphere CLI can collaborate to streamline artifact management and export data systematically. By following the steps provided, users can extend or adapt the code to suit their requirements.
